subject: Sociology
title: Correlation vs Causation
description: A short lesson to help you apply Correlation vs Causation.

hook.text: Ice cream sales and drowning deaths both spike in summer. Ice cream doesn't cause drowning. Heat causes both.

buildup.text: Correlation means two things move together. Causation means one actually produces the other.

discovery.text: Spurious correlations happen when a hidden third variable drives both. Sociology is full of these traps.

twist.text: Media constantly confuses the two. Headlines like 'coffee drinkers live longer' rarely mention wealth as a factor.

climax.text: Good sociological research controls for confounding variables. Without controls, any finding could be an illusion.

punchline.text: Two things happening together doesn't mean one caused the other.

quiz.question: What is a spurious correlation?
options: When a hidden third variable drives both observed trends
When two variables directly cause each other
When no correlation exists at all
